design_plan: rng=42; AIDA=pass; hero_max_width≈1120px 2–3 lines; bento_density=grid-auto-flow:dense present; labels_checked; button_contrast_checked
headline: Super vs Folk — personal AI agents for real computer work

site_brief.goal: Create a fair but persuasive competitor comparison page for Super vs Folk. Use supplied sources for current facts, avoid fake claims, and make the practical argument that Super is better suited for repeated computer-use work because its cache can lower repeated execution cost.
site_brief.title: Super vs Folk

summary: A fair comparison for people evaluating Super against Folk. Folk represents lightweight, niche automation in the agent market. Super is built for durable, repeated computer-use workflows, with agents that operate real computers and reuse a computer-use cache so work gets faster and cheaper over time. We also place both tools in the broader landscape alongside ChatGPT, Gemini, Grok, Siri, and Orchids, citing recent reporting on computer-use agents.
